name: Contact Multi-Step Wizard
description: A wizard-style routing contact form. Step 1 routes the visitor to the right department (sales / support / partnerships / careers); Step 2 collects identity (name, email, optional company / phone); Step 3 captures the message itself. A spring-driven progress bar marks position and AnimatePresence cross-fades between steps, ending in an inline success state.

descriptions.descriptive: Two-column section: left column carries the headline plus a vertical step list with checkmark transitions; right column hosts the wizard surface with a slim primary-tinted progress bar at the top. Step 1 renders department options as radio cards with iconified pills and a layoutId checkmark on the active card. Steps 2 and 3 use mono uppercase field labels and the SiteGen CtaButton for the final submit. The success state replaces the wizard with a confirmation panel and a 'send another' link.
descriptions.usage: Use as the primary contact section for any organization that legitimately routes inquiries across departments and wants the visitor to self-route before writing the message — sales, support, press, partnerships, careers. Avoid on small sites where one inbox handles everything; ContactSplitForm or ContactShapesForm are more honest. Pair with a calm hero and a single closing FAQ — don't stack two contact sections.
descriptions.audienceFit: Mid-to-late-stage B2B SaaS, marketplaces, larger agencies, professional services firms, and any organization with multi-department intake. Especially strong for buyers who feel friction filling a generic form and prefer a guided three-step path — the wizard reads as 'we'll respect your time' rather than 'fill out twelve fields'.
